title: Crispy Dutch Pork Stew
description: A rich and crispy one-pot pork stew with coconut milk and rendered fat, perfect for a hearty dinner.
instructions: Heat the rendered pork fat in a Dutch oven over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the pork cubes, browning them on all sides for about 5 minutes.
Remove the browned pork and set aside; in the same fat add diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until translucent, 3 to 4 minutes.
Stir in sliced red bell pepper and cook for 2 minutes.
Return the pork to the pot, then add tomato paste, smoked paprika, cumin, oregano, and stir to coat.
Pour in coconut milk and chicken stock, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot and let the stew cook for 45 minutes, or until pork is tender.
Increase heat to medium-high, uncover, and reduce the liquid until it thickens slightly, about 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then stir in lime juice if using.
Ladle the stew into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley before serving.
